records.ety: [OE. pite, OF. pit, piti, F. piti, L. pietas piety, kindness, pity. See Pious, and cf. Piety.]

defs: A feeling for the sufferings or distresses of another or others; sympathy with the grief or misery of another; compassion; fellow-feeling; commiseration.
quotes: He that hath pity upon the poor lendeth unto the Lord.
He . . . has no more pity in him than a dog.

defs: A reason or cause of pity, grief, or regret; a thing to be regretted.
quotes: What pity is it That we can die but once to serve our country!
notes: ☞ In this sense, sometimes used in the plural, especially in the colloquialism: “It is a thousand pities.”
synonyms: and therefore requiers a certain degree of equality in situation
to its fullest exercise. Compassion is deep tenderness for another under severe or inevitable misfortune. Pity regards its object not only as suffering

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Pitied (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Pitying.]

defs: To feel pity or compassion for; to have sympathy with; to compassionate; to commiserate; to have tender feelings toward (any one), awakened by a knowledge of suffering.
quotes: Like as a father pitieth his children, so the Lord pitieth them that fear him.

defs: To move to pity; -- used impersonally.
quotes: It pitieth them to see her in the dust.

defs: To be compassionate; to show pity.
quotes: I will not pity, nor spare, nor have mercy.
